name: customize_kernel
short_description: Customize a baseline RTL kernel by assigning values for parameters on the top module to create a new customized kernel while keeping the baseline IP definition the same. The customized kernel cannot be incrementally customized again.
full_help:
customize_kernel

Description: 
Customize a baseline RTL kernel by assigning values for parameters on the top module to create a new customized kernel while keeping the baseline IP definition the same. The customized kernel cannot be incrementally customized again.

Syntax: 
customize_kernel  -xo_path <arg> [-output_xo_path <arg>] -kernel_name <arg>
                  [-output_kernel <arg>] [-ip_params <args>]
                  [-ip_params_file <arg>] [-target_board <arg>] [-quiet]
                  [-verbose]

Returns: 
Path to destination object file

Usage: 
  Name               Description
  ------------------------------
  -xo_path           Absolute path to the object file that contains the named
                     kernel
  [-output_xo_path]  Write results to the given .xo file, leaving input file 
                     unmodified
  -kernel_name       Kernel to customize
  [-output_kernel]   A kernel name. Must differ from the value specified by 
                     -kernel_name
  [-ip_params]       Customize RTL based IP used by the kernel. A list of 
                     config name/value pairs.
  [-ip_params_file]  Customize RTL based IP used by the kernel. Reads input 
                     from file.
  [-target_board]    Customize DCP based IP used by the kernel. Add the 
                     support for the target board.
  [-quiet]           Ignore command errors
  [-verbose]         Suspend message limits during command execution

Categories: 
Vitis, Acceleration, FileIO
